perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of its Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, hire or solicit any employee of the Company or encourage any such employee to leave such employment or hire any such employee who has left such employment, except pursuant to a general solicitation which is not directed specifically to any such employees; provided, that nothing in this Section 1 shall prevent Kennedy or any of his Affiliates from hiring (i) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company before the Merger, (ii) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company after the Merger one-hundred eighty (180) days from the date of termination of employment; or (iii) Jesse Miramontes (a current employee of the Company) and any Kennedy family member (including, without limitation, Nancy Kennedy (wife) or John C. Kennedy, IV (son) who are currently employed by the Company).
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restriction on Hiring, Kennedy shall not permit any of its Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, hire or solicit any employee of the Company or encourage any such employee to leave such employment or hire any such employee who has left such employment, except pursuant to a general solicitation which is not directed specifically to any such employees; provided, that nothing in this Section 1 shall prevent Kennedy or any of his Affiliates from hiring (i) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company before the Merger, (ii) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company after the Merger one-hundred eighty (180) days from the date of termination of employment; or (iii) Jesse Miramontes (a current employee of the Company) and any Kennedy family member (including, without limitation, Nancy Kennedy (wife) or John C. Kennedy, IV (son) who are currently employed by the Company).
perturbation.explanation: By changing “Restricted Period” to “Restriction on Hiring,” and not updating later references, the time period for non-solicitation becomes ambiguous. Section 7(d) references “Restricted Period” creating in-text contradiction on the intended meaning and scope of the Hiring restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) This Agreement shall be effective as of the Closing Date of the Merger. If the Closing does not occur, this Agreement shall be void and of no further force and effect and there shall be no liability on the part of any party hereto, except that the provisions of Section 2 shall survive. The duties and obligations of Kennedy, with respect to any rights accruing to Company under this Agreement during the term of the Restricted Period and thereafter, shall survive any termination of the Restricted Period.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Restricted Period', while the changed text replaces it with 'Restriction on Hiring'. The contradicted text refers to the 'Restricted Period', which is now undefined due to the change. This creates a direct conflict because the duration and scope of the restrictions on Kennedy's hiring practices are now ambiguous, potentially rendering Section 7(d) unenforceable and thus causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of his Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, solicit or entice, or attempt to solicit or entice, any clients or customers of the Company or potential clients or customers of the Company for purposes of diverting their business or services from the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restriction on Solicitation, Kennedy shall not permit any of its Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, solicit or entice, or attempt to solicit or entice, any clients or customers of the Company or potential clients or customers of the Company for purposes of diverting their business or services from the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Restricted Period” to “Restriction on Solicitation” creates a discrepancy. The defined “Restricted Period” of three years is not reflected in the new term. This could lead to disputes over the duration of the non-solicitation clause. This is further emphasized in Section 1 (f) where the original verbiage is present.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) If Kennedy violates any of the provisions of this Section 1, the computation of the time period provided in this Section 1 shall be tolled from the first date of the breach until (i) the date judicial relief is obtained by the Company, (ii) the Company states in writing that it will seek no judicial relief for said violation, or (iii) Kennedy provides satisfactory evidence to the Company that such breach has been terminated.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Restricted Period" to "Restriction on Solicitation" creates a contradiction. The explanation states the "Restricted Period" is defined as three years, which is not reflected in the new term. The contradicted text refers to Section 1 which uses the original "Restricted Period". By using the new term, the time period computation referenced in the contradicted text becomes unclear as it's unclear how the Restriction on Solicitation can be tolled if it isn't properly defined.

perturbation.original_text: Kennedy acknowledges that the restrictions contained in this Section are reasonable and necessary to protect the legitimate interests of the Company. In the event that any covenant contained in this Section should ever be adjudicated to exceed the time, geographic, product, or service, or other limitations permitted by applicable law in any jurisdiction, then any court is expressly empowered to reform such covenant, and such covenant shall be deemed reformed, in such jurisdiction to the maximum time, geographic, product or service, or other limitations permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: Kennedy acknowledges that the restrictions contained in this Section are reasonable and necessary to protect the legitimate interests of the Business. In the event that any covenant contained in this Section should ever be adjudicated to exceed the time, geographic, product, or service, or other limitations permitted by applicable law in any jurisdiction, then any court is expressly empowered to reform such covenant, and such covenant shall be deemed reformed, in such jurisdiction to the maximum time, geographic, product or service, or other limitations permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Company” to “Business” in this acknowledgement shifts the focus. While the 'Company' is defined, the 'Business' is not. This creates ambiguity in the scope of protection intended by the non-compete. The text is contradicting the definition of “Company” earlier in the agreement. The Recitals refer to both the "Autocam Business" and the "Company Business", further confusing what is being protected by this Section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS WHEREAS, Company and Autocam Corporation, a Michigan Corporation (“Autocam”), have entered into that certain Agreement and Plan of Merger dated as of the date hereof (the “Merger Agreement”), under which PMC Global Acquisition Corporation, a Michigan corporation and a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company, will merge with and into Autocam, and Autocam will continue as a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company (the “Merger”); WHEREAS, Kennedy owns a majority of shares of Autocam before the Merger, received consideration from the Company in connection with the Merger, and is a stockholder of the Company; WHEREAS, Autocam engages in the precision metal components business for the transportation industry (the “Autocam Business”); WHEREAS, the Company engages in the precision metal components business for the automotive industry, aerospace industry, industrial machinery, precision shafts, small tool components, and other industrial and consumer products using precision metal components and assemblies (the “Company Business”) throughout the world; WHEREAS, Kennedy has had access to knowledge of Autocam’s trade secrets and other confidential and proprietary information and will have access to knowledge of the Company’s trade secrets and other confidential and proprietary information and that it is of vital importance to the success of the Company for Kennedy (i) not to compete against the Company and its business and activities for a specified period of time after the Merger and (ii) not to disclose Company’s trade secrets and other proprietary information; and WHEREAS, capitalized terms not defined herein shall have the meaning ascribed to them in the Merger Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Company" to "Business" introduces a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because "Company" is a defined term in the Recitals, whereas "Business" is not explicitly defined and could refer to either "Autocam Business" or "Company Business", creating ambiguity. This contradicts the intention of the original text and creates a strong contradiction within the document regarding the scope of the non-compete agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Except as may otherwise be required by law, Kennedy agrees not to, without the Company’s prior written consent, (i) disclose or transfer any Proprietary Information to any Person other than the Company or (ii) use any Proprietary Information for any unauthorized purpose, either during the term of this Agreement or the Restricted Period, unless and until such Proprietary Information has become available to the public generally without fault by Kennedy.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as may otherwise be required by law, Kennedy agrees not to, without the Company’s approval, (i) disclose or transfer any Proprietary Information to any Person other than the Company or (ii) use any Proprietary Information for any unauthorized purpose, either during the term of this Agreement or the Restricted Period, unless and until such Proprietary Information has become available to the public generally without fault by Kennedy.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “prior written consent” to “approval” introduces uncertainty. 'Consent' generally implies an explicit, affirmative agreement documented in writing. 'Approval' could be interpreted more broadly, potentially including implied or verbal approval. This ambiguity is critical in a legal context where explicit agreements are preferred. This is because section 7(f) is being affected, where, '...All notices, requests, consents, claims, demands, waivers, and other communications hereunder shall be in writing...' is being affected, due to the new term being used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) All notices, requests, consents, claims, demands, waivers, and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been given (i) when delivered by hand (with written confirmation of receipt); (ii) when received by the addressee if sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier (receipt requested); (iii) on the date sent by facsimile or e-mail of a PDF document (with confirmation of transmission) if sent during normal business hours of the recipient, and on the next Business Day if sent after normal business hours of the recipient or (iv) on the third (3rd) day after the date mailed, by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid. Such communications must be sent to the respective parties at the following addresses If to Company, to: NN, Inc. 2000 Waters Edge Drive Johnson City, TN 37604 Attention: James H. Dorton Facsimile: 423-743-7670 Email: james.dorton@nninc.com 4 With a copy to (which shall not constitute notice): Husch Blackwell LLP 4801 Main St., Suite 1000 Kansas City, MO 64112 Attention: John D. Moore Facsimile: 816 983-8000 Email: john.moore@huschblackwell.com If to Kennedy, to: John C. Kennedy 4162 East Paris Avenue, SE Kentwood, MI 49512 Facsimile No.: 616-698-6876 Email: JKENNEDY@Autocam.com With a copy to (which shall not constitute notice): Law Weathers, PC 333 Bridge Street, Suite 800 Grand Rapids, MI 49504 Attention: Tony Barnes Facsimile: 616-913-1222 Email: tbarnes@lawweathers.com
perturbation.justification: The change from "prior written consent" to "approval" introduces ambiguity as 'approval' could be interpreted more broadly than explicit written consent. This directly contradicts section (f) which specifies that '...All notices, requests, consents, claims, demands, waivers, and other communications hereunder shall be in writing...' This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. Since approval can be verbal, it breaks compatibility with section (f)'s requirement that all consents be in writing, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to the covenants and representations set forth in Sections 1, 2, and 3 of this Agreement, the “Company” shall include (i) any corporation, partnership, limited liability company, 3 or other business entity of which an aggregate of 50% or more of the outstanding voting stock, membership interests, or other ownership interests are at any time directly or indirectly owned by the Company, (ii) Autocam, and (iii) the Subsidiaries.
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to the covenants and representations set forth in Sections 1, 2, and 3 of this Agreement, the “Organization” shall include (i) any corporation, partnership, limited liability company, 3 or other business entity of which an aggregate of 50% or more of the outstanding voting stock, membership interests, or other ownership interests are at any time directly or indirectly owned by the Company, (ii) Autocam, and (iii) the Subsidiaries.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “Company” to “Organization” only in the definition section, we introduce significant confusion. Throughout the entire document 'Company' is used. Now, the extension of the non-compete and non-disclosure obligations becomes ambiguous. Is Kennedy restricted from competing with just NN, Inc., Autocam, and their Subsidiaries, or also any broader 'Organization' that NN, Inc. might be related to? Section 4 is affected, where the text makes direct contact with the term "Company", but is no longer being enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Necessity of Covenants; Injunctive Relief . Kennedy acknowledges that a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement would give rise to irreparable harm to the Company, for which monetary damages would not be an adequate remedy, and hereby agrees that in the event of a breach or a threatened breach by Kennedy of any such obligations, the Company shall, in addition to any and all other rights and remedies that may be available to it in respect of such breach, be entitled to equitable relief, including a temporary restraining order, an injunction, specific performance, and any other relief that may be available from a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Company' with 'Organization' in the definition related to sections 1, 2, and 3. This introduces ambiguity because the rest of the document, including the contradicted section regarding Injunctive Relief, refers to the 'Company.' The change creates a contradiction because the Injunctive Relief clause specifically protects the 'Company,' but the definition of 'Company' relevant to that clause is now altered, potentially excluding entities that were originally included. This misalignment directly affects the enforceability of the Injunctive Relief clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the term which the clause applies to is unclear.
